calendar.matters:
ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Conveyance Of Property
Off Calendar Mar-27-2009 Off Calendar

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar May-29-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Edward Koplowitz for Elena Dwinnell (Deceased). Mark Shustoff for Michael Shah (Administrator for Elena Dwinnell's estate). Richard Shearer for Gary Weiner (Nicolas Yugoff estate). Robert Fletcher, Jr. for the Treasury Tax Collector. Jonathan Ferdon for the San Francisco Public Adminstrator ('SFPAS"). Paul Yugoff in propria persona.

The Parties presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to the Mr. Yugoff's request for a continuance which the Court denied, the issue regarding the assessment on the 23rd Street property and Mr. Yugoff's surcharges. The Court will allow Mr. Fletcher time to investigate and brief the assessment issue. Mr. Flethcer's brief is due by April 6, 2009. Counsel's responses are due by April 13, 2009. Mr. Ferdon will prepare, circulate and submit the order to the Court leaving the dollar amounts blank. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR#7077. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy B. Mann.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Conveyance Of Property
Off Calendar Jan-28-2009 Continued To Mar-27-09 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Mar-25-2009 Continued To Mar-27-09 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Conveyance Of Property
Off Calendar Jan-07-2009 Continued To Mar-27-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Richard Shearer for Gary Weiner. Jonathan Ferdon for the San Francisco Public Administrator. Ed Koplowitz for Elena Dwinell.

Mr. Ferdon stated that the SFPA's accounting was done, that he was fine tuning the account from the property Manager regarding rents collected and that he had received information from Mr. Bassi regarding rents collected by his client for the Sonoma property. In addition, Mr. Ferdon stated that he was working on the hours spent on the case, that he was planning on filing the accounting as best he could and that he would send copies to all Parties. Mr. Koplowitz stated that this matter had been going on for a long time and suggested setting a hearing date for Mr. Ferdon's accounting petition to be heard. Mr. Shearer concurred with Mr. Koplowitz as to setting a hearing date. Mr. Yugoff requested an advance of funds for living expenses. Mr. Koplowitz stated that he was not in agreement with a preliminary distribution and that if allowed, the other Beneficiaries should also receive a distribution. Mr. Shearer made it clear that he was not in agreement with the distribution. The Court directed Mr. Yugoff to speak to Mr. Ferdon regarding the advance request. The matter was continued to March 25, 2009 at 9:00 a.m. to be heard by Judge Dearman for the hearing on Mr. Ferdon's accounting. The location of the courtroom is to be determined.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: Jackie Alameda/Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Bobby Mann.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Conveyance Of Property
Off Calendar Sep-24-2008 Continued To Jan-07-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ed Koplowitz for Elena Dwinnell (decedent). Jonathan Ferdon for the San Francisco Public Administrator (SFPA). Richard Shearer for Gary Weiner (Personal Representative of the Estate of Nickolas Yugoff). Paul Yugoff in propria persona.

Mr. Ferdon stated that he had received nothing from Mr. Yugoff or Ms. Dwinnell's Estate, that Mr. Shustoff had informed him that Ms. Dwinnell's house had been broken into, and that he wasn't sure if he was going to be able to provide him with any information. In addition, he stated that he would contact the personal representative for the Elena Dwinnell Estate and will try to do the accounting by the end of the year. Mr. Koplowitz was in agreement with a direct distribution, and that he wanted to preserve his ability to remove Mr. Yugoff as Trustee in the event that there are any issues. In addition, he requested presenting the issue regarding the offset of rents at the time of distribution. Mr. Yugoff turned over various documents regarding account records and copies of checks for 1998 and 1999 to Mr. Ferdon in open Court. The matter was continued to January 7, 2009 for further status.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Karen Farnsworth, CSR#5162. Clerk: Rosie Noguera/ J. Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Conveyance Of Property
Off Calendar Aug-27-2008 Continued To Sep-24-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Richard Shearer for Gary Weiner (Personal Representative for the Estate of Nikolas Yugoff). Edward Koplowitz for Elena Dwinnell. Jonathan Ferdon for the San Francisco Public Administrator (SFPA). Paul Yugoff in propria persona.

Mr. Ferdon reported that all of the properties had been sold, that escrow on the Dolores Street property closed, and that he was waiting for all the parties to turn over information regarding all assets. In addition, he requested that the Court expand the previous order to include the personal representative for the estate of Elena Dwinnell to preserve all documents and turn over information. The Court directed Mr. Yugoff to submit all documents regarding tax returns and loans under penalty of perjury to the Court on or before September 10, 2008. The Court continued the matter to September 24, 2008 for the last time. If parties do not submit any additional documentation, Mr. Ferdon will file the petition for final distribution based on what he has which the Court is prepared to accept.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera/ J. Alameda Bailiff: Deputy W. Yee.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Conveyance Of Property
Off Calendar Jul-16-2008 Continued To Aug-27-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jonathan Ferdon for the San Francisco Public Administrator. Donna Quan for Gary Weiner. Paul Yugoff in Pro Per.

Mr. Ferdon stated that he did not have the accountings on the properties held by Nikolas and Elena, that he did not have the information on the trust matters and that he was unable to do anything without that information. He also suggested Mr. Yugoff provide any information he had on the matter. Ms. Quan stated that she was waiting for information from the bank on the trust accounts. Mr. Yugoff stated that he needed access to Elena's belongings to gather information. The matter was continued to August 27, 2008 for further status.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy F. Smith.

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (1090 Dolores St, San Francisco)
Off Calendar Jul-09-2008 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jonathan Ferdon for the San Francisco Public Administrator. Paul Yugoff (Trustee) in Pro Per.

Mr. Yugoff requested the matter be continued until after the July 16, 2008 hearing. Mr. Ferdon stated that this was the sale that the Court ordered, that the hearing was on the Estate of Ms. Dwinnell, the Decedent's daughter, that the house was not in the Trust and that the other parties wanted distribution. The Court denied Mr. Yugoff's request. The sale was confirmed at $1,450,000.00. Bond was waived. The Court granted the petition, (subject to a written order).

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy K. Sandberg.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Conveyance Of Property
Off Calendar Jun-04-2008 Continued To Jul-16-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Michael Bassi for Gary Weiner (Personal Representative of the Nickolas Yugoff Estate). Ed Koplowitz for Elena Dwinnell. Paul Yugoff in Por Per. Jonathan Ferdon for the San Francisco Public Administrator.

Mr. Ferdon submitted the status report to the Court and stated that he provided copies to all parties. In addition, he reported that the McPeek property sold, that escrow closed, that he received a bid on the Dolores Street property that was close to 90% of the appraised value and that he had requested a re-appraisal that came in at $1,595,000.00. He also stated that he was still waiting for the accountings, that the proceeds from the sale would go to the Tassia trust, that Mr. Yugoff was the Trustee of that Trust, and that the only thing left to do was to petition the Court for instructions on how to proceed. Mr. Koplowitz reported that Ms. Dwinnell passed away on Saturday and asked about the rents that had been collected. Mr. Ferdon stated that Mr Yugoff turned over one check for one months rent, and that there were no accountings for the Sanchez Street property or the Dolores Street property. Mr. Yugoff stated that he sent two checks to Keynotes Properties, that a full accounting had been submitted and that a supplement was filed accounting for all the rents. Mr. Bassi stated that his client was in the process of making an investigation as to the assets from the Tassia estate. The Court advised Mr. Yugoff to comply with the Court's order. The matter was continued to July 16, 2008.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Maria Lertora CSR#11935. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy F. Aquino.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Conveyance Of Property
Off Calendar Apr-09-2008 Continued To Jun-04-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jonathan Ferdon from the San Francisco Public Administrator. Ed Koplowitz for Elena Dwennell (Beneficiary). Michael Bassi for Gary Weiner. Paul Yugoff in Pro Per.

Mr. Ferdon reported that the family and Mr. Yugoff were cooperationg and that he had 2 open houses. In addition, he stated that he had an opening bid of $35,000.00 for the Sonoma property, that he sent notice, but that he did not receive any responses/objections and that he would proceed with the sale. Mr. Koplowitz and Mr. Bassi stated that their clients had no objections. Mr. Ferdon also stated that the items that were turned over by Mr. Yugoff were not the items that were being requested by Ms. Dwennell (jewelry/coins), and submitted a report that was prepared by the appraiser showing the value at $340.00 for costume jewelry. Mr. Koplowitz requested that Mr. Yugoff's 1/3 share be retained. The Court directed Mr. Yugoff to file a supplement in regards to rents he had collected and ordered Mr. Yugoff to turn over all collected rents to Mr. Ferdon. The matter was continued to June 4, 2008 for further status.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Cabrera.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Conveyance Of Property
Off Calendar Jan-29-2008 Continued To Apr-09-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jonathan Ferdon from the San Francisco Public Adminisrtator (SFPA). Ed Kaplowitz for Elena Dwinnell (Benificiary). Machael Bassi for Gary Weiner. Paul Yugoff in Pro Per.

Mr. Ferdon stated that he was working towards the McPeek property and that they were looking at late February for open bids. In addition, he reported that he had received no information from Mr. Yugoff regarding the personal property, that he was ready to proceed with bids on the property without viewing, and that he found a Connect General account. Mr. Yugoff presented a canvus bag to Mr. Ferdon stating that the coins and jewlery were in it, and hand delivered a copy of the suplemantal accounting to him. Mr. Ferdon took possession of the bag and stated that he would have the contents appraised. Mr. Kaplowitz stated that Mr. Yugoff should hire Counsel to assist him with the accounting. Mr. Bassi expressed that the Public Administrator had the right to enter the property. The Court ordered Mr. Yugoff to open the property for viewing every Tuesday starting April 1st, from 1:00 p.m. to 4:00 p.m until the property is sold. Mr. Ferdon will prepare the order. The matter was contiuned to April 9, 2008 for further status. If everything works out, there is no need to appear.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Helen Sorep-Matley CSR 225. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Lee.

PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar Dec-21-2007 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ed Koplowitz for Elena Dwinnell (Beneficiary). Mike Bassi for Gary Weiner. Jonathan Ferdon for the San Francsico Public Administrator (SFPA).

Mr. Ferdon stated that the order had been approved and that some changes were made. Mr. Bassi expressed his objection stating that Mr. Ferdon was requesting that his client account for a period of time in which he was not appointed, that his client had no auhtority to gain information on the bank accounts, and that it was easier for the SFPA to obtain that information. The Court suggested Mr. Bassi submit a declaration. Mr. Ferdon argued that it was the responsibility of the Successor Administrator to get the accounting from the Former Administrator, that an accounting was needed from Paul Yugoff, Ms. Dwinnell and Nicholas, and that an accounting needed to be done by Steven for the property sold in Sonoma County. Mr. Koplowitz reported that Ms. Dwinnell was in bad health, that she was not able to do a formal accounting, but they were willing to file a declaration regarding the loan. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR #11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Lewis.

PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar Nov-28-2007 Continued To Dec-19-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ed Kaplowitz for Elena Dwinnell. Jonathan Ferdon for the San Francisco Public Administrator. Mike Bassi for the Nicholas Yugoff Estate. Paul Yugoff in Pro Per.
Mr. Ferdon seeks the ability to go forward and close the estate. He stated that he was having problems with the Beneficiaries not turning over the property of the estate, and that it was difficult to figure things out to the last penny. Mr. Yugoff agreed to set up a meeting for an appraisal of the personal property. An accounting is still needed from Nicholas (now deceased). Elena still has property belonging to the estate. Mr. Bassi stated that the property in Sonoma needed to be sold for whatever they could get without court confirmation, that the Dolores Street property should be sold, that the court could allocate credits and debits, and that he objected to Mr. Yugoff holding property as the Trustee. Mr. Koplowitz agreed with Mr. Bassi's position. Mr. Yugoff asked the Court to affirm the Trust that named him as the Trustee. The Court ordered the property be sold, and stated that Mr. Yugoff had the right of first refusal. Mr. Ferdon is to prepare the order as to the effect of the right of first refusal and the sale of the property. Mr. Koplowitz has a problem with that stating that Mr. Yugoff would scare off potential buyers. Mr. Bassi and Mr. Koplowitz questioned Mr. Yugoff's ability to buy the Dolores St. property. Mr. Bassi stated that a procedure needed to be worked out if Mr. Yugoff is to buy the property. The Court ordered the Yugoff family to cooperate with the sale of the Dolores St. property, and the Sonoma County property be sold without Court confirmation. The Court directed Mr. Yugoff to turn over the funds collected and the personal property. All issues were continued to December 19, 2007.
Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Arlene Howell CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Lee and Deputy Smith.

PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar Nov-20-2007 Continued To Nov-28-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Edward Kaplowitz for Elena Dwinnell. Jonathan Ferdon for the San Francisco Public Administrator. Paul Dimitri Yugoff in Pro Per. Mike Bassi for the Personal Representative of the Nicholas Yugoff estate.

Mr. Ferdon requested that the matter be heard by Judge John Dearman due to his familiarity with the matter. The Court continued to November 28, 2007.

Judge Pro Tem: Dorothy L. McMath. Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR#11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiffs: R. Guitron and R. Leong.

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (10185 Martinelli Road, Forestville, Ca 95436)
Off Calendar Mar-24-2006 Written Order Received
MINI MINUTES: Jonathan Ferdon appearing for Public Administrator. Paul Demitri Yugoff appearing for the trust. The estate has a 50% interest. The other 50% interest joins in the sale. Bid is increased to $245,000.00 for 100% interest. Court is confirming the sale for 50% interest in the estate. Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR 11722. Clerk: Ella Yip.
